code: uri <- tempfile() sto <- storr_tiledb(uri, init = TRUE) uri2 <- tempfile() dr <- driver_tiledb(uri2, init = TRUE) sto2 <- storr::storr(dr)

code: dat <- head(mtcars, 2)  # set sto$set("a", dat) sto$set("b", list(name = "John")) sto$mset(c("a", "b", "c"), value = list(dat, dat, dat), namespace = "ns1")  # get sto$get("b") # $name # [1] "John" sto$mget(c("b", "b"), namespace = c("objects", "ns1")) # [[1]] # [[1]]$name # [1] "John" #  #  # [[2]] #               mpg cyl disp  hp drat    wt  qsec vs am gear carb # Mazda RX4      21   6  160 110  3.9 2.620 16.46  0  1    4    4 # Mazda RX4 Wag  21   6  160 110  3.9 2.875 17.02  0  1    4    4  # list keys sto$list("objects") # [1] "a" "b" sto$list("ns1") # [1] "a" "b" "c"  # list namespaces sto$list_namespaces() # [1] "ns1"     "objects"  # list hashes sto$list_hashes() # [1] "c184c6034d956360b5bb682fcd4b6cb8" "fd441562b6f1ec33e42a4369820cb0ae"  # del sto$del("a") sto$del("b") sto$exists("a") # [1] FALSE  # Retrieve hashes and namespaces sto$list_namespaces() # [1] "ns1" sto$list_hashes() # [1] "c184c6034d956360b5bb682fcd4b6cb8" "fd441562b6f1ec33e42a4369820cb0ae"  # Delete unused hash sto$gc() sto$list_hashes() # [1] "c184c6034d956360b5bb682fcd4b6cb8"  # Delete by namespace sto$clear("ns1") # [1] TRUE sto$list("ns1") # character(0)

title: API Usage
text: key-namespace pair, can attached notes expiration metadata. Set key metadata creating key: update/retrieve $set_keymeta() $get_keymeta() methods: Expiration management Clear metadata
code: # Expires in 5 minutes sto$set("aa", 1, expires_at = Sys.time() + 60 * 5,          notes = "{\"name\":\"John\",\"id\":12345}")  # Expires in 1 second sto$set("bb", 1, expires_at = Sys.time() + 1) # Retrieve key metadata sto$get_keymeta("aa", use_cache = FALSE) sto$mget_keymeta(c("aa", "bb"), use_cache = TRUE)  # Update key metadata sto$set_keymeta("bb", notes = "Updated Note") sto$get_keymeta("bb") # Retrieve keys with expiration sto$keys_with_expiration() #    namespace    key          expires_at #       <char> <char>              <POSc> # 1:   objects     aa 2026-04-20 10:55:26 # 2:   objects     bb 2026-04-20 10:50:27  Sys.sleep(1)  # Retrieve expired keys sto$expired_keys() #    namespace    key          expires_at #       <char> <char>              <POSc> # 1:   objects     bb 2026-04-20 10:50:27  # Remove expired keys sto$clear_expired_keys()  # Check for expired keys sto$has_expired_keys() # [1] FALSE  # Or via gc() sto$gc(clear_expired = TRUE) sto$clr_keymeta("aa") sto$get_keymeta("aa") # $expires_at # [1] NA #  # $notes # [1] NA

title: API Usage
text: writing data TileDB, generates multiple array fragments disk. time, files need consolidated order improve query performance reducing /O overhead. Note consolidation operation delete old fragments; creates new consolidated fragments. actual cleanup old fragments performed separate vacuum operation. manage storr TileDB fragments use storr_fragments() encapsulates consolidation vacuum operations. Consolidate everything (arrays) Now, let’s clean old fragments.
code: # Set up a new storr uri <- tempfile() sto <- storr_tiledb(uri, init = TRUE)  # Set 3 keys and 2 objects sto$set("a", 1) sto$set("b", 2) sto$set("c", 1) # Initialise StorrFragments class fosto <- storr_fragments(uri)  # Total fragments (5, 3 for keys, 2 for hashes) fosto$frag_num() # [1] 5  # Total fragments to vacuum fosto$to_vacuum_num() # [1] 0 # Consolidate all fosto$consolidate() # [1] TRUE # Total fragments (2, 1 for keys, 1 for hashes) fosto$frag_num() # [1] 2  # Total fragments to vacuum (5) fosto$to_vacuum_num() # [1] 5 # Cleaup old fragments fosto$vacuum() # [1] TRUE  # Total fragments to vacuum (0) fosto$to_vacuum_num() # [1] 0

title: Data Model
text: Content Addressable Storage (CAS) system using TileDB underlying storage consists collection two Sparse Arrays: one maps keys hashes another maps hashes objects. Specifically, driver storage TileDB Group two member TileDB Arrays stored relative Group’s URI path.

title: Data Model
text: Members tbl_keys (array): maps key-namespace pairs hashes (expiry /notes, optional) tbl_data (array): maps hashes values (serialised R objects) Metadata hash_algo: name hash algorithm. type: Group identifier, "storr"

title: Data Model
text: tbl_keys - 2D sparse array maps key-namespace pairs hashes key-metadata. Dimensions: namespace (ASCII) key (ASCII) Attributes: hash (ASCII), expires_at (DATETIME_MS) notes (UTF8) tbl_data - 1D sparse array maps hashes object values. Dimensions: hash (ASCII) Attributes: value (ASCII) TileDB datatypes parentheses.

what: Can I change the storr’s hash algorithm?
title: FAQ
text: Yes, using driver’s active field $hash_algorithm: Change hash algorithm follows: Reopen storr pick new hash algorithm:
code: # URI path uri <- tempfile()  # Create a storr with "sha256" digest sto <- storr_tiledb(uri, init = TRUE, hash_algorithm = "sha256")  sto$set("a", 1)  sto$get_hash("a") # [1] "55d98602c58516fba3b169ccfebb0edc98fe5af34f86f82822125c4521273da3" # Initialise the driver dr <- driver_tiledb(uri)  # Check its hash algo dr$hash_algorithm # [1] "sha256"  # Change hash digest to 'md5' dr$hash_algorithm <- 'md5'  dr$hash_algorithm # [1] "md5" # Open new storr instance sto <- storr_tiledb(uri)  sto$set("a", 1)  # key 'a' is hashed with 'md5' sto$get_hash("a") # [1] "38e42db36c4414f7bbc19d750f71a721"

what: How can I rehash the entire key-value storr?
title: FAQ
text: need create new storr desired hash algorithm, export source storr new one. Now, export source destination storr using class method $export_tdb():
code: # source storr uri <- tempfile() sto <- storr_tiledb(uri, init = TRUE) sto$set("a", 1) sto$get_hash("a") # [1] "38e42db36c4414f7bbc19d750f71a721" # create a storr with new hash algorithm uri_dest <- tempfile() sto_dest <- storr_tiledb(uri_dest, init = TRUE, hash_algorithm = "blake3") sto$export_tdb(uri_dest = uri_dest)  # check sto_dest$set("a", 1) sto_dest$get_hash("a") # [1] "235cda51f465ddead90eb33b8aa23d485dfa7bc6b7cbd642ef3f06ecc8197569"

title: Quick Reference
text: storr_tiledb() supports storing keys asynchronous parallel via mirai framework.
code: # Set asynchronously (non-blocking) sto$set_async("key3", 2, namespace = "_session") sto$set_async("key1", 1, namespace = "ns1") sto$set_keymeta_async("key2", notes = "key1-note")  Sys.sleep(2)  # Retrieve key, keymeta sto$get("key3", "_session") # [1] 2 sto$get_keymeta("key2", "objects") # $expires_at # [1] NA #  # $notes # [1] "key1-note" # clear default namespace sto$clear() # [1] TRUE  # set keys in parallel vals <- 1:3 keys <- paste0("aa", vals)  ma <- mapply(function(k, v) {    sto$set_async(k,                 value = v,                 use_cache = FALSE)   },   k = keys, v = vals, SIMPLIFY = FALSE)   Sys.sleep(2)  sto$index_export(namespace = "objects")[, 1:3] #    namespace    key                             hash #       <char> <char>                           <char> # 1:   objects    aa1 9dc695ac953ca975b83c673f7144cffb # 2:   objects    aa2 574f34320260479985d0afc302223d8d # 3:   objects    aa3 54227b57594b584567dfb54b10047d9b

title: Quick Reference
text: TileDB backend supports encryption: Note ’ve created context using R6.tiledb::new_context() instead tiledb_tiledb_ctx(), latter stores context package cache. Now, create storr encryption:
code: # Requires a TileDB Context with encryption configuration parameters key <- "5b643a5e173c27d76b3f2af01fcb327b" config <- tiledb::tiledb_config() config["sm.encryption_type"] <- "AES_256_GCM"; config["sm.encryption_key"] <- key ctx <- R6.tiledb::new_context(config) # tiledb::tiledb_ctx(config) # Create a storr with context that encapsulates encryption configuration uri_enc <- tempfile() stoe <- storr_tiledb(uri_enc, init = TRUE, context = ctx)  stoe$set("a", 1) stoe$get("a")  # [1] 1  # No access without the key stoe_new <- storr_tiledb(uri_enc) # Error: # ! GenericTileIO: Error reading generic tile; tile is encrypted with AES_256_GCM but given key is for NO_ENCRYPTION  # Pass the context with encryption parameters stoe_new <- storr_tiledb(uri_enc, context = ctx) stoe_new$get("a") # [1] 1

title: Quick Reference
text: Create storr cloud storage:
code: # Set context with AWS S3 config parameters # NB: store values in ENV or in credential store, i.e., via keyring package config <- tiledb::tiledb_config()  config["vfs.s3.region"] <- Sys.getenv("AWS_DEFAULT_REGION") config["vfs.s3.aws_access_key_id"] <- Sys.getenv("AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID") config["vfs.s3.aws_secret_access_key"] <- Sys.getenv("AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY") ctx <- R6.tiledb::new_context(config) # Create a remote storr uri <- 's3//bucket/my-storr' sto <- storr_tiledb(uri, init = TRUE, context = ctx)

title: TileDB Storr Driver — driver_tiledb
text: underlying structure similar driver_dbi(); content-addressed database consists two (array) tables. Specifically, driver storage TileDB Group two member Arrays stored relative Group's URI path. next sub-sections describe group structure array data models. Group Members tbl_keys (array): maps key-namespace pairs hashes (expiry /notes, optional) tbl_data (array): maps hashes values (serialised R objects) Metadata hash_algo: name hash algorithm. type: Group identifier, "storr" Arrays tbl_keys - 2D sparse array maps key-namespace pairs hashes key-metadata. Dimensions: namespace (ASCII) key (ASCII) Attributes: hash (ASCII), expires_at (DATETIME_MS) notes (UTF8) tbl_data - 1D sparse array maps hashes object values. Dimensions: hash (ASCII) Attributes: value (ASCII) TileDB datatypes parentheses.
